page: Amazon Web Services r8g.metal-24xl is a bare-metal, memory-optimized server powered by 96 dedicated physical cores of the ARM64-based AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor. Operating at a clock speed of 2.8 GHz, the CPU features 64 KB L1d cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 36 MB L3 cache. The system is equipped with 768.0 GB of DDR5 memory, providing a high resource density of 8.0 GB of RAM per core. It lacks local storage and dedicated GPU hardware, relying on network-attached storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 40 Gbps.

Benchmark results place this server in the top tier for multi-core processing and memory bandwidth. The stress-ng multi-core test and Geekbench multi-core evaluations both rank in the top 10% of tested systems. Memory performance is exceptionally strong, with read-only large block bandwidth reaching 481.15 GB/sec and write large block bandwidth at 218.18 GB/sec. Database operations and Redis SET operations also achieve top-tier status, with Redis processing 198.34 million operations per second.

While multi-threaded and memory-bound tasks perform in the top tier, single-core performance is more moderate, with stress-ng single-core results falling in the average tier. Additionally, while LLM text generation for medium and large models is strong, text generation for small models (135M) is poor, landing in the bottom 10%. Given the high DDR5 memory capacity, dedicated ARM64 cores, and top-tier database benchmark scores, this bare-metal instance is suited for memory-intensive workloads, large-scale database operations, and high-throughput web serving, though it is less optimal for workloads requiring local NVMe storage or GPU acceleration.
description: Amazon Web Services r8g.metal-24xl is a memory-optimized, bare-metal server featuring 96 dedicated physical cores on the ARM64 AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 architecture. Operating at 2.8 GHz, the processor is paired with 768.0 GB of DDR5 memory, yielding 8.0 GB of RAM per core. The server lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs, but provides a 40 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. In benchmark testing, the instance delivers top-tier multi-core CPU performance and memory bandwidth, with read-only large block speeds of 481.15 GB/sec and top-tier Redis SET throughput. However, single-core performance remains average, and small-model LLM text generation is poor. This configuration is highly efficient for memory-intensive applications, large-scale database operations, and static web serving where high memory capacity and dedicated physical cores are required, offering a balanced resource-to-compute ratio without the overhead of virtualization.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r8g.metal-24xl is a bare-metal, memory-optimized server with 96 dedicated ARM64 Graviton cores, 768 GB DDR5 RAM, and top-tier multi-core and memory bandwidth performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r8g.metal-24xl is a bare-metal server with 96 ARM64 cores, 768 GB DDR5 RAM, and top-tier database and memory bandwidth.
tagline: A bare-metal ARM64 platform featuring ninety-six dedicated cores and massive DDR5 memory bandwidth for memory-intensive database workloads.
bullet_points: 96 dedicated ARM64 AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 physical cores at 2.8 GHz
768.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory (8.0 GB per core)
Top-tier multi-core CPU, memory bandwidth, and Redis SET performance
40 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage or GPU
Suited for large-scale database operations and memory-intensive workloads
Average single-core performance and poor small-model LLM text generation
